title: Precision structured glass articles, integrated circuit packages, optical devices, microfluidic devices, and methods for making the same
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a reconstituted wafer- and/or panel-level package comprising a glass substrate having a plurality of cavities. Each cavity is configured to hold a single IC chip. The reconstituted wafer- and/or panel-level package can be used in a fan-out wafer or panel level packaging process. The glass substrate can include at least two layers having different photosensitivities with one layer being sufficiently photosensitive to be capable of being photomachined to form the cavities.
claims:
Claims 1. A structured glass article comprising: a glass substrate comprising a glass cladding layer fused to a glass base layer; and one or more cavities formed in the glass cladding layer of the glass substrate. 2. The structured glass article of claim 1, wherein the glass cladding layer has a higher etch rate in an etchant than the glass core layer. 3. The structured glass article of claim 1, wherein the photosensitivity of the glass cladding layer is greater than the photosensitivity of the glass base layer. 4. The structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the glass base layer defines a floor of the one or more cavities. 5. The structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein a floor of the one or more cavities comprises a surface roughness of at most about 20 nm. 6. The structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein a difference between a first depth of one of the one or more cavities at a first position along the perimeter of the cavity and a second depth of the one of the one or more cavities at a second position along the perimeter of the cavity opposite the first position is at most about 5 Î¼Î¹Î·. 7. The structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the glass cladding layer defines sidewalls of the one or more cavities. 8. The structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein sidewalls of the one or more cavities are substantially straight. 9. The structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein an angle formed between a sidewall and a floor of the one or more cavities is at most about 30Â°. 10. The structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 9, wherein a difference between a first width of one of the one or more cavities at a top of the cavity and a second width of the one of the one or more cavities at a bottom of the cavity is at most about 1 mm. 11. The structured glass article of claim 10, wherein a depth of the one of the one or more cavities is at least about 50 Î¼ÏÎ·. 12. A packaged integrated circuit comprising: the structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 11; and an integrated circuit chip disposed within the one or more cavities. 13. The packaged integrated circuit of claim 12, wherein a width, a length, or a depth of the cavity is within 20 microns of a width, a length, or a depth, respectively, of the integrated circuit chip positioned in the cavity. 14. The packaged integrated circuit of claim 12 or claim 13, comprising a redistribution layer. 15. The packaged integrated circuit of claim 14, comprising a ball grid array. 16. A reconstituted wafer- and/ or panel-level package comprising the packaged integrated circuit of any of claims 12 to 15. 17. An optical device comprising the structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 11. 18. A microfluidic device comprising the structured glass article of any of claims 1 to 11. 19. A method for forming a structured glass article, the method comprising: applying a mask to a surface of a glass substrate comprising a glass cladding layer fused to a glass core layer such that the mask is disposed on the glass cladding layer, the mask comprising one or more open regions at which the glass substrate remains uncovered by the mask, the glass cladding layer having a higher etch rate in an etchant than the glass core layer; and 

 exposing the masked glass substrate to the etchant, thereby selectively etching a portion of the glass cladding layer and forming one or more cavities in the glass substrate. 20. A method for making a reconstituted wafer- and/ or panel-level package, comprising: forming a plurality of cavities in a glass substrate comprising a glass cladding layer fused to a glass base layer; and positioning an integrated circuit chip in each of the plurality of cavities in the glass substrate.
